channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Southampton lose appeal against play-off expulsion over Spygate
item.description: Southampton lose their appeal against expulsion from the Championship play-off final for spying.

item.title: Aston Villa have new heroes as they win first major trophy for 30 years
item.description: Aston Villa beat Freiburg in Istanbul to lift the Europa League trophy for the first time.

item.title: Prince William celebrates 'amazing night' as Villa fans party
item.description: Prince William shows a range of emotions during Aston Villa's Europa League final win over Freiburg in Istanbul, as fans celebrate in Birmingham.

item.title: Chess, lectures & ignoring noise: Emery masters Europe again
item.description: Unai Emery delivers Aston Villa their first trophy in 30 years with a 3-0 win over Freiburg in the Europa League final. BBC Sport takes a look at the man behind Villa's famous victory.

item.title: Villa keep alive English hopes of six Champions League spots
item.description: Aston Villa's Europa League win against Freiburg keeps alive the Premier League's chances of having six teams in next season's Champions League.

item.title: 'The first in my lifetime' - Arsenal fans rejoice after shedding 'bottlers' tag
item.description: Arsenal fans young and old celebrate winning the Premier League title after three consecutive second-placed finishes.

item.title: A just or harsh punishment? Reaction to Southampton expulsion
item.description: Southampton will be playing in the Championship next season after they were expelled from the play-offs following their admission that they spied on three league rivals this season.

item.title: Major-minor nations split in World Cup qualifying
item.description: Uefa will create a two-tier qualification process for the 2030 World Cup which will mean major countries will no longer play minor nations such as San Marino, Gibraltar and Andorra.

item.title: Empty rooms and Fifa cancellations - US hotels fear World Cup washout
item.description: The 2026 Fifa World Cup was supposed to provide a tourism boom for the United States, but now the fear is that may never materialise.

item.title: Real eye Rodri amid Guardiola exit - Thursday's gossip
item.description: Real Madrid are confident of signing Rodri from Manchester City amid Pep Guardiola's departure, Bayern Munich plan a shock move for John Stones, Atletico Madrid want Marc Cucurella, plus more.

item.title: Man Utd-Forest referee not selected for final day
item.description: The referee for Manchester United's win over Nottingham Forest last Sunday has not been selected for any match in the final round of Premier League fixtures.

item.title: Boats, fire and a TikTok song - inside Arsenal's title win
item.description: The inside story of how Mikel Arteta turned Arsenal from 'nearly men' into Premier League winners.

item.title: What is at stake on final day of Premier League season?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains what is at stake on the final day of the 2025-26 Premier League season.

item.title: What tactics might Alonso use at Chelsea?
item.description: What tactical approach will Xabi Alonso bring to Stamford Bridge next season after being announced as Chelsea boss?

item.title: Toulouse make approach for Motherwell boss Askou
item.description: Toulouse have made an official approach to Motherwell as they aim to appoint manager Jens Berthel Askou as their new head coach.

item.title: Hearts criticise 'irresponsible' pitch invasion remarks
item.description: Hearts have hit out at what they call "highly irresponsible" comments with "dangerous implications" about the Celtic Park pitch invasion, after Celtic boss Martin O'Neill dismissed claims the incident embarrassed Scottish football as "nonsense".

item.title: The crucial split-second call that could deny Spurs safety
item.description: A controversial split-second call leaves Tottenham are left fighting for Premier League survival and dignity to avoid the ultimate humiliation on the last day of the season, says chief football writer Phil McNulty.

item.title: Saka calls out critics as players celebrate title at Emirates at 5am
item.description: Arsenal have had to wait 22 years to celebrate a title win, and both the club's players and fans made sure to savour every second of it.

item.title: Ranking the best European trophy-winning managers
item.description: Unai Emery has the chance to win a fifth Europa League on Wednesday, but where does he rank among the best European trophy winners?

item.title: Arsenal win Premier League as Man City held at Bournemouth
item.description: Arsenal are crowned champions of the Premier League for the first time in 22 years after Manchester City are held to a 1-1 draw at Bournemouth at the Vitality Stadium, a result which secures European football for the home side for the first time in their history.

item.title: Tottenham lose at Chelsea to take relegation fight to final day
item.description: Tottenham miss the opportunity to secure Premier League safety after they're beaten 2-1 by Chelsea at Stamford Bridge, meaning their fight against relegation will go down to the final day, with the club sitting two points above the bottom three.

item.title: 'Exceptional' Arsenal celebrate as Premier League title is secured
item.description: Watch the moment Arsenal players and staff celebrate as they secure their first Premier League title in 22 years after Manchester City draw 1-1 at Bournemout

item.title: 'Let me talk to my chairman' - Guardiola coy on future after Bournemouth draw
item.description: Pep Guardiola remained tightlipped on his future after Manchester City's 1-1 draw at Bournemouth - a result that sees Arsenal win the Premier League for the first time in 22 years.

item.title: Going, going, gone... what happens at Man City when Guardiola departs?
item.description: Speaking on Match of the Day, Danny Murphy and Joe Hart discuss what Manchester City do now after missing out on the Premier League title, along with strong reports that manager Pep Guardiola will leave the club in the summer.

item.title: 'It's been messy' & 'no winners among fans' - play-off final chaos
item.description: Having been reinstated into the Championship play-off final after Southampton's expulsion, BBC Radio Tees' Mark Drury lays out Middlesbrough's 'messy' preparations.

item.title: Meet the 26 players in Scotland’s first men's World Cup squad since 1998
item.description: Boss Steve Clarke has named his squad for this summer's World Cup - meet the 26 players.

item.title: Southend sack Maher two days after Wembley win
item.description: Southend United sack head coach Kevin Maher two days after the club's historic FA Trophy win at Wembley.

item.title: Quiz: Can you name every Europa League winner?
item.description: As Aston Villa beat Freiburg in the Europa League final to end their 30-year trophy drought, we ask - can you name all of the competition's previous winners?

item.title: North Korean side wins in South to reach final
item.description: A team of North Korean footballers who made history by crossing the border into South Korea earned a victory to take them into the Asian Women's Champions League final.

item.title: The key moments that decided the Premier League title race
item.description: BBC Sport looks at the key moments that helped turn Arsenal from nearly men to league winners.

item.title: World Cup 2026: Every nation's squad as they are announced
item.description: Every squad for the 2026 World Cup in the United States, Canada and Mexico as they are announced.

item.title: Scotland call up young quartet for pre-World Cup training
item.description: Hearts duo James Wilson and Liam McFarlane, Dundee defender Luke Graham and Manchester United midfielder Tyler Fletcher will train with Scotland next week as the national team prepare for the World Cup.

item.title: Wrexham star wants Championship play-offs restart
item.description: Wrexham are monitoring the Southampton spygate scandal before considering any potential legal action.

item.title: Spain midfielder Lopez ruled out of World Cup
item.description: Spain midfielder Fermin Lopez is ruled out of the World Cup with a broken bone in his foot.

item.title: Rotherham appoint McClaren to head of football role
item.description: Former England boss Steve McClaren takes up a newly-created role as head of football at Rotherham, recently relegated to League Two.

item.title: Xhaka makes fourth Switzerland World Cup squad
item.description: Sunderland's Granit Xhaka will take part in his fourth successive World Cup after being included in the Switzerland squad for this summer's tournament.

item.title: Title despair & Guardiola going - 24 hours of pain for Man City
item.description: Manchester City's world fell apart in the space of 24 hours after surrendering the Premier League title and the bombshell news of manager Pep Guardiola's impending departure.

item.title: Runners-up no more - How Arsenal's patience paid off with Arteta
item.description: Speaking on Match of the Day, Danny Murphy and Joe Hart praise Arsenal for giving Mikel Arteta time after he guided the club to their first Premier League title in 22 years, following Manchester City's 1-1 draw with Bournemouth.

item.title: 'So much on the line' - Spurs struggles sets up final day relegation decider
item.description: Joe Hart and Danny Murphy discuss how Tottenham's lack of clincal finishing saw them miss an opportunity to secure Premier League safety and analyse who will avoid relegation with Spurs and West Ham in danger of going down on the final day of the Premier League season.

item.title: 'I can't ask for more' - Iraola delighted as Bournemouth secure Europe
item.description: Andoni Iraola says "he couldn't ask for more" as Bournemouth secure European football for the first time in their history with a 1-1 draw against Manchester City. The game was the Spaniard's final home match in charge, before he leaves in the summer.

item.title: 'They've deserved it' - How brilliant Bournemouth reached Europe - analysis
item.description: Speaking on Match of the Day, Danny Murphy and Joe Hart praise Bournemouth's tactics and attitude as their 1-1 draw against Manchester City secures European football for the first time in their club's history.

item.title: Head coach to manager? Chelsea's key change puts Alonso in control
item.description: Speaking on Match of the Day, Danny Murphy and Joe Hart analyse Chelsea's decision to appoint Xabi Alonso.

item.title: 'We can reach our target' - De Zerbi defiant after Chelsea defeat
item.description: Tottenham manager Roberto De Zerbi says he was pleased with side's performance, despite their 2-1 defeat at Chelsea, a result which means their fight against relegation from the Premier League will go to the final day.

item.title: 'We needed to fight' - McFarlane proud as Chelsea beat Tottenham
item.description: Chelsea interim head coach Calum McFarlane praised his side's resilience after their FA Cup final defeat as they bounced back in the Premier League to beat Tottenham 2-1 to keep alive their hopes of qualifying for European football next season.

item.title: The games that defined Guardiola's Premier League titles
item.description: BBC Sport looks back at the defining moments of Pep Guardiola's six Premier League titles, following reports that he is set to depart Manchester City after Sunday's final game of the season.

item.title: He's changed the face of English football - Rooney on Guardiola
item.description: Wayne Rooney, Kelly Somers and Kae Kurd discuss the influence Pep Guardiola has had on English football and consider the impact his expected departure from Manchester City will have on the Premier League.

item.title: Pitch invasions 'a bad look' - but Clarke praises Tartan Army
item.description: National head coach Steve Clarke says fans spilling on to the pitch to celebrate in domestic games is "a bad look" for Scottish football but praises the Tartan Army for avoiding such scenes.

item.title: World Cup player signs petition calling for protection from extreme heat
item.description: Norway midfielder Morten Thorsby says "everybody in football loses" if Fifa does not do more to protect players from extreme heat at the World Cup.

item.title: After firing Man City to title - is Shaw best striker in WSL history?
item.description: After Khadija Shaw helped Manchester City win the Women's Super League with another superb season, BBC Sport looks at where she stands among the division's greatest strikers.

item.title: Bournemouth in Europe for first time - but Champions League still on
item.description: Bournemouth secure their place in Europe for the first time with a draw against Manchester City, but the Champions League is still within reach.

item.title: Who has made Troy's Premier League team of the week?
item.description: After every round of Premier League matches this season, Troy Deeney gives us his team of the week. Do you agree with his choices?

item.title: Five reasons why Best was a genius
item.description: George Best would have been 80 on 22 May. Take a look back on an unforgettable career and enjoy five of his most memorable goals.

item.title: 'I was screaming' - footballer stabbed seven times recalls mass knife attack
item.description: Footballer Jonathan Gjoshe was among the 11 passengers seriously injured in a knife attack on a train as it travelled through Cambridgeshire last November.

item.title: Stories, scheduling & spectacle - five takeaways from 2025-26 WSL
item.description: With the 2025-26 season in the books, BBC Sport answers five pertinent questions from the Women's Super League.

item.title: Third place secured - what challenges are ahead for Carrick?
item.description: The manager's job is his, so what are the key tasks in front of Michael Carrick at Manchester United?

item.title: Ups, downs and the race for Europe
item.description: All you need to know about promotion, relegation and qualification for Europe in England and Scotland's top men's football leagues, and the Women's Super League.

item.title: All done deals since February, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Who are the oldest players to feature at a World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who the oldest players are in the history of the World Cup

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Who has the most Premier League assists in a season?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at who has provided the most assists in the history of the Premier League.

item.title: What does Premiership climax mean for European places?
item.description: What does Saturday's dramatic and chaotic climax to the Scottish Premiership top-six mean for European football next season?

item.title: All you need to know about the Europa League final
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at all you need to know before this year's Europa League final.

item.title: What is financial gap between Old Firm and rest of Scottish Premiership?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at the financial gap between the Old Firm and the rest of Scottish top-flight football.

item.title: What are Fifa's rules on switching nationalities?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the rules on players switching nations in international football.

item.title: Emslie recalled by Scotland five months after giving birth
item.description: Scotland have recalled Claire Emslie for next month's Women's World Cup qualifying double-header against Israel, five months on from the forward giving birth.

item.title: Your guide to the Premiership play-off final
item.description: St Mirren and Partick Thistle will meet over two matches to determine who will play in the Scottish Premiership next season. Here is your guide.

item.title: Scotland boss Clarke set for new deal until Euro 2028 - gossip
item.description: Scotland head coach Steve Clarke is set to sign a new contract, while Hearts plan a summer move for Falkirk winger Calvin Miller.

item.title: Man Utd midfielder Toone returns to England squad
item.description: Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone returns to the England squad for the crucial Women's World Cup qualifiers against Spain and Ukraine.

item.title: Women's coach given lifetime ban for secret filming
item.description: Czech coach Petr Vlachovsky, who secretly filmed female football players in changing rooms and showers, is handed a lifetime ban by Uefa.

item.title: Focus on errors can 'tarnish' goalkeepers - Hampton
item.description: England's Hannah Hampton says media can "tarnish the reputation" of female goalkeepers by focusing on mistakes and not their positive displays.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.
